name: Sariel
description: Sariel, also spelled Suriel, is an archangel figure appearing in various Jewish and Christian apocryphal texts and esoteric traditions. Unlike some archangels with clearly defined roles, Sariel's attributes and functions vary across different sources, leading to some ambiguity and even negative portrayals. He is often associated with healing, particularly in some Kabbalistic traditions, where he is invoked for protection against illness. However, in other texts, such as the Book of Enoch, Sariel is listed as one of the fallen angels who taught humanity forbidden knowledge, leading to corruption and the eventual flood. This duality makes Sariel a complex figure, embodying both benevolent and potentially destructive forces. His connection to the moon and lunar cycles is also documented in some esoteric circles. The ambiguity surrounding Sariel's role warrants further exploration in comparative religious studies, examining the evolution of angelic figures and the interplay of good and evil within Abrahamic traditions.
subtitle: Angel of Healing and Knowledge

keyMyths.title: The Fall of Sariel
keyMyths.description: In some versions of the Book of Enoch, Sariel is depicted as one of the Grigori, or Watchers, who descended to Earth and cohabitated with human women. He is accused of teaching humanity forbidden knowledge, such as astrology and the use of herbs for potentially harmful purposes. This act of disobedience is seen as a catalyst for corruption and divine punishment, marking Sariel as a fallen angel and a source of earthly suffering. His actions lead to the great flood, cleansing the Earth of sin.

keyMyths.title: Sariel the Healer
keyMyths.description: Contrasting the Enochian narrative, Kabbalistic traditions often portray Sariel as an angel of healing. He is invoked in rituals and prayers for protection against illness and for the restoration of health. In this role, Sariel is seen as a benevolent force, working to alleviate suffering and to maintain the balance of the natural world. His association with healing is often linked to his connection with divine wisdom and understanding of the human body.

keyMyths.title: Sariel and Lunar Cycles
keyMyths.description: Some esoteric traditions associate Sariel with the moon and its cycles. This connection highlights the cyclical nature of life, death, and rebirth. Sariel's influence is said to be strongest during certain lunar phases, making him a key figure in divination and magical practices. The moon's reflective light symbolizes Sariel's ability to illuminate hidden truths and to guide individuals through periods of darkness and uncertainty. This association further solidifies his role as a mediator between the divine and the mortal realms.

sources.author: Davidson, Gustav
sources.work: A Dictionary of Angels, Including the Fallen Angels
sources.description: Comprehensive overview of angels in Jewish and Christian traditions, including Sariel's various roles and associations.
